title: zea mays subsp. ceratina
intro: Zea mays subsp. ceratina, also known as teosinte, is an ancient subspecies of the maize plant native to Central America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of teosinte include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and improve digestion. Teosinte can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or supplement, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
